content.problemStatement:
There are NNN cards on a table, out of which XXX cards are face-up and the remaining are face-down.

In one operation, we can do the following:


Select any one card and flip it (i.e. if it was initially face-up, after the operation, it will be face-down and vice versa)
What is the minimum number of operations we must perform so that all the cards face in the same direction (i.e. either all are face-up or all are face-down)?


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the total number of cards and the number of cards which are initially face-up.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of cards you must flip so that all the cards face in the same direction.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and X, where N is the total number of cards and X is the number of cards that are initially face‑up.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer – the minimum number of flips required so that all cards face the same direction. Print each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: all cards are already face‑down, so 0 flips.
Test case 2: 2 up, 2 down → flip either group, minimum 2 flips.
Test case 3: all cards are face‑up, so 0 flips.
Test case 4: 2 up, 8 down → flip the 2 up cards.
Only one card which is face‑down; already uniform.
Three cards are up and three are down; flipping either side needs 3 flips.

solution.approach: To make all cards face the same direction we have two choices: flip all face‑up cards (X flips) or flip all face‑down cards (N‑X flips). The optimal answer is the smaller of these two numbers, i.e. min(X, N‑X). This can be computed independently for each test case in O(1) time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        X = int(next(it))
        out.append(str(min(X, N - X)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
